definitions: Used to indicate that one does not know the answer to a question.
Used to express that one has no more information or insight than the person asking the question.
examples: "When will the new store open?" "Your guess is as good as mine."
"Why did he quit?" "Your guess is as good as mine; he never said."
"Will it rain tomorrow?" "Your guess is as good as mine, the forecast keeps changing."
lemma: your guess is as good as mine
source: your guess is as good as mine
